title: The sudden dharna: How Congress surprised its own MPs, govt with protest outside PM's residence
content: Congress leaders staged a protest at Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence. They demanded the resignation of the Prime Minister and education minister. Many party leaders were unaware of the protest plan beforehand. Police forcibly removed Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi from the site. The government sent officials to persuade the opposition leaders to leave.

title: Sreeshankar eyes golden leap at CWG 2026: 'Hopefully change the colour of medal'
content: Murali Sreeshankar is preparing for upcoming athletic events after a serious injury. He aims to improve upon his previous silver medal performance. Sreeshankar is currently training in Poland to adapt to track conditions. He is focusing on recovery and peaking at the right times. The long jumper is optimistic about achieving a significant jump in major championships.

title: Donald Trump's tariff tornado returns as India deal stalls
content: Donald Trump plans to impose new tariffs on many countries, including India. These tariffs will affect global trade and financial markets significantly. India's trade deal negotiations with the US face potential complications. The US administration cites unfair trade practices and forced labor concerns. This move escalates trade tensions and impacts international commerce.

title: Europe’s biggest tech firm is offering employees $20K-plus bonus if they stay until 2030
content: ASML is reportedly offering employees a significant retention bonus. Eligible employees worldwide will receive a conditional share award valued at twenty thousand euros. This award will be granted on January 1, 2027, and will vest on January 1, 2030. The company is experiencing continued growth and development, investing in innovation and expansion. This move contrasts with US companies which have seen substantial layoffs this year.

title: 'How to kill, dispose bodies': Bengaluru triple murder accused turned to AI, say cops
content: Bengaluru police are delving into a disturbing triple murder involving an AI chatbot. Reports reveal that the prime suspect meticulously plotted the murders over six months, using AI to seek counsel on execution tactics, body disposal techniques, and cleaning traces of the crime. Financial issues and family discord appear to have driven the heinous act. The couple involved was captured after attempting to flee to Puducherry.

title: India buys more Russian, Latin American crude as Middle East supplies dip
content: India stepped up imports from Moscow once Washington granted a temporary waiver, helping offset reduced supplies from Gulf producers caused by the disruption of traffic through the strategically important Strait of Hormuz.

title: Aamir Khan’s half-brother struggles with schizophrenia and homelessness, claims ex-wife Eva Grover
content: Actress Eva Grover revealed her past marriage to Hyder Ali Khan involved domestic violence. She stated his schizophrenia and alcohol use worsened their relationship significantly. Grover endured abuse for five years before initiating divorce proceedings. She was separated from her daughter for ten years after the divorce. Grover also mentioned Aamir Khan was aware of her marital difficulties.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: 'It's embarrassing': Argentina's World Cup final conduct under fresh scrutiny
content: New footage shows an alleged attempt to influence the referee after Enzo Fernandez's red card.  Argentina full-back Nicolas Tagliafico appeared to feign injury moments after the foul occurred.  Lionel Messi then rushed towards the referee, pointing to the fallen Tagliafico.  This effort did not change the referee's decision regarding Fernandez's dismissal.  The match concluded with Spain winning their second World Cup title.

title: Bee brains reveal how colonies coordinate without a central controller
content: Honeybee colonies organise work without any leader assigning tasks, but scientists have now discovered that specific brain circuits help control this process. By changing the activity of a gene-linked neural circuit, researchers made older worker bees return to caring for the queen, a job usually done only by younger bees. The findings offer new insight into how bees coordinate complex social behaviour.

title: 11-year-old reads to shelter dogs and cats to help them feel calmer
content: An 11-year-old Florida boy, Nehemiah Turner, has completed 100 hours of reading to rescue animals at the Jacksonville Humane Society. Through the shelter’s Pawsitive Reading programme, he spent two years reading books to dogs and cats waiting for adoption. Nehemiah became the first child to reach the milestone, earning recognition for helping comfort shelter animals and supporting their journey towards finding new homes.

title: Iran’s IRGC claims it has ‘destroyed’ Amazon’s central data hub in Bahrain
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed missile strikes on Amazon's Bahrain data infrastructure.  These attacks were reportedly in retaliation for recent US strikes on Iranian civilian sites.  The IRGC also stated it targeted US air defense systems in Bahrain.  Earlier this year, Amazon's cloud operations in Bahrain suffered damage from an Iranian strike.  Iran's mission to the UN called for condemnation of US actions at a nuclear site.

title: A look at Leonardo DiCaprio's multi-million-dollar property portfolio
content: Leonardo DiCaprio boasts a remarkable real estate portfolio that spans the United States and Belize. His assets feature a lavish compound in Hollywood Hills, a tranquil private island in Belize, chic apartments in New York City, and a sprawling estate in Palm Springs. Furthermore, DiCaprio has acquired a stunning Spanish Colonial home in Los Feliz, along with a picturesque beachfront property in Malibu and a stylish residence in Beverly Hills.

title: 'I'm so sorry': Emiliano Martinez apologises, hints at Argentina retirement
content: Emiliano Martinez suggested his international career might be ending after Argentina's World Cup final defeat.  He expressed deep regret and the need for reflection on his future with the team.  Martinez delivered an exceptional performance despite playing with a broken finger.  His remarkable 11 saves set a new record for a World Cup final match.  Spain ultimately secured a 1-0 victory in extra time after a hard-fought contest.

title: WWE RAW: Solo Sikoa offers Seth Rollins alliance as Bloodline dominates
content: The July 20 WWE Monday Night RAW event in Detroit featured several exciting tag team and singles matches. Bron Breakker and Austin Theory secured a victory in the opening tag team bout. Oba Femi defeated JD McDonagh and challenged Brock Lesnar to a future match. LA Knight faced Jacob Fatu in the main event, with Fatu emerging victorious.

title: Lamine Yamal's girlfriend Inés García sparks debate with latest update
content: Lamine Yamal has been in a high profile relationship with popular social media influencer, Inés García Santos, for the last few months. As Spain won the 2026 World Cup, their relationship has sparked outrage among fans.

title: Dylan Larkin likely to stay with Red Wings despite trade speculation
content: Trade speculation about Detroit Red Wings captain Dylan Larkin remains. But recent developments suggest a deal may not be imminent. Negotiations have been complicated by changes in the leadership of the organization and reported trade demands, but insiders think the franchise could keep Larkin through the regular season. The uncertainty also raises new questions about Detroit’s captaincy and long-term roster plans.

title: 'Went where they least expected us': Priyanka Gandhi on 'Chalo PM House' protest
content: Congress leaders marched to Prime Minister's residence demanding his and the Education Minister's resignation.  They protested brutalities against students and demanded accountability for examination irregularities.  Police detained several senior Congress leaders during the demonstration in New Delhi.  Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi met injured students admitted at Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital.  The party stated students' demands are clear regarding paper leaks and ministerial responsibility.

title: Gates Foundation concludes Epstein review, approves new governance measures
content: The Gates Foundation conducted an external review that concluded there was no evidence of misconduct related to its interactions with Jeffrey Epstein. The foundation emphasized that its main dealings were centered on a potential fund and grants for polio eradication. In response, its Governing Board unanimously approved new governance measures, including centralized vetting and stronger conflict-of-interest policies, reaffirming its commitment to trust and transparency in its operations.

title: 'I can hit from ball one': Tilak Varma hits back at strike-rate criticism
content: Tilak Varma addressed criticism regarding his strike rate during recent T20I tours. He stated he bats according to team requirements and match situations. India's top-order collapses often necessitated rebuilding innings for the middle order. As vice-captain, Varma prioritizes fulfilling team management's specific responsibilities. He emphasized playing for the country involves significant responsibilities.

title: Watch: Rahul Gandhi dragged away, forcibly removed from protest site; pics show nosebleed
content: Congress released videos showing dramatic scenes of senior leaders' detention. Rahul Gandhi was seen lying on the road during the protest. Priyanka Gandhi Vadra resisted boarding a police bus as she was pulled inside. Police forcibly removed and detained several opposition leaders outside the Prime Minister's residence. The protest occurred over the NEET issue and alleged police action on students.

title: From gym to hangar to sleeping quarters: Iran's three-missile strikes on US base in Jordan
content: An Iranian missile attack tragically claimed the lives of  US service members at a Jordanian air base, targeting their sleeping quarters. This shocking incident exposed critical shortcomings in the protection of American forces. Following the strike, US President Donald Trump committed to retaliation, while Iran's supreme leader issued a stark warning about consequences for continued aggression.

title: No apology: Argentina's Paredes breaks silence after World Cup final chaos vs Spain
content: Leandro Paredes addressed the World Cup final chaos without apology. FIFA has initiated a disciplinary investigation into the post-match incidents. Television footage showed Paredes involved in altercations with Spanish players. Argentina's team also faced criticism for their actions after the match. The investigation could lead to retrospective sanctions for those involved.

title: AAP's 'jugalbandi' swipe as Rahul Gandhi protests near PM's residence; Congress responds
content: The AAP alleged a partnership between the BJP and Congress parties.  This followed a Congress sit-in protest outside Prime Minister Modi's residence.  Congress leaders demanded resignations from key government ministers.  They declined an outreach attempt by Union minister Jitendra Singh.  The CJP has been protesting at Jantar Mantar since June 21.

title: 'E20 petrol may cut mileage by up to 5%, but no damage to engines': Govt tells Parliament
content: In a written reply in the Rajya Sabha, Minister of State for Petroleum and Natural Gas Suresh Gopi said fuel economy is influenced by several factors, including driving style, vehicle maintenance, tyre pressure, wheel alignment and the use of air conditioning. The government said the drop in mileage for some older vehicles is generally limited to around 3 to 5 per cent.

title: 'Conditions were challenging': Tilak Varma reveals what went wrong for India
content: Tilak Varma discussed India's recent T20I series losses against Ireland and England. He cited adapting to overseas conditions as a significant challenge for the team. Varma emphasized the importance of staying calm and fostering a positive team environment. The team has learned valuable lessons from these difficult overseas campaigns. India aims to regain momentum by executing these learnings in upcoming matches.

title: From eligibility to taxes: What NRIs need to know before buying a home in India
content: Non-Resident Indians can now easily buy residential property in India.  Payments must flow through recognized banking channels, and cash deals are prohibited.  Banks offer loans to NRIs on terms similar to resident buyers.  The PMAY subsidy scheme is not directly available to NRIs.  Affordable housing options are emerging in various Indian cities.

title: 'Ghor paap': PM Modi on NEET paper leak; vows strict action against culprits
content: More than 170 people, including CJP protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said. According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC).

title: Justin Langer frontrunner for England coach job; Shastri, Dravid not in the race
content: Former Australia coach Justin Langer has emerged as the frontrunner to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test coach. While the ECB hopes to make an appointment before next month's Pakistan series, former India coaches Ravi Shastri and Rahul Dravid are not part of the recruitment process.

title: 'Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai' to return on OTT; R Madhavan to host
content: The beloved talk show 'Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai' is making a comeback on Zee5, reimagined as 'Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai 2.0' with R. Madhavan as the host. Madhavan emphasizes the show's ability to forge authentic human connections that transcend the digital sphere. This new season will spotlight remarkable individuals and trendsetters who have made their mark on the world stage.

title: Another oil shock loading for India? How Red Sea disruptions may hit Russian crude supply
content: Iran-backed Yemeni group, the Houthis, have threatened a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. What this essentially means is that Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports are set to be hit. And India which gets a big portion of its crude imports from the Middle East country.

title: 'Criticising him isn't justified': Sikandar Raza defends Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi can become a generational talent. He urged patience for the 15-year-old after his challenging debut series. Raza highlighted the importance of proper management for the young cricketer's development. Zimbabwe anticipates an entertaining T20I series against India. Both teams are in transition, making the contest more evenly balanced.

title: Turkey's main opposition splits as Ozgur Ozel launches new party
content: Turkey's opposition leader Ozgur Ozel announced his departure from the Republican People's Party. He and his allies are forming a new political party after a court ruling. This move follows a court decision that reinstated former leader Kemal Kilicdaroglu. Ozel stated the new party marks a hopeful new beginning for opposition politics. The split could potentially strengthen President Erdogan's long-standing grip on power.

title: 'My first target will be to wear the India jersey': Ashok Sharma eyes dream debut
content: Fast bowler Ashok Sharma received his maiden senior national team call-up. He hopes to make his international debut during the upcoming T20I series. Sharma aims to take wickets and play as many matches as possible. He also dreams of representing India in Test cricket soon. Dale Steyn remains his biggest inspiration for fast bowling.

title: Tracking threats before they reach border: India's next line of defence taking shape in space
content: India is deploying a 52-satellite constellation for enhanced surveillance. This program aims to bolster national security and maritime domain awareness. Private firms will build 31 satellites, while ISRO constructs the remaining twenty-one. The constellation will provide persistent intelligence and secure communications across vast areas. This initiative strengthens India's defense capabilities against evolving threats.

title: How constant niggles and breakdowns are hampering 2027 ODI World Cup plans
content: India's injury list is growing: Nitish Kumar Reddy injured himself during the ODI series against Afghanistan last month, Hardik Pandya has yet to feature in an ODI for India since the Champions Trophy final against New Zealand in March 2025. Harshit Rana has suffered two injury setbacks in the last six months. Washington Sundar has had a couple of breakdowns in the same period. Varun Chakravarthy suffered a hairline fracture before a Grade 2 hamstring injury ruled him out of action.

title: The future of flying? US speeds up plans for air taxis, supersonic planes and drones
content: The FAA is updating aviation rules to encourage new technologies.  It supports air taxis and supersonic passenger flights with new regulations.  Pilot programs are preparing for the safe integration of electric air taxis.  Companies are developing charging infrastructure for electric aircraft operations.  The agency also aims to expand drone use and commercial space launches.

title: 'When I needed someone, he was there': PV Sindhu recalls Kohli's role in comeback
content: PV Sindhu received crucial guidance from Virat Kohli during a difficult career period.  This conversation helped her overcome a serious injury and focus on rehabilitation.  Her husband facilitated her recovery away from public scrutiny in Atlanta.  Sindhu then achieved a historic Japan Open women's singles title win.  This victory marked a remarkable comeback after her extensive recovery process.

title: Qatar Airways denied boarding to infant; court awards family Rs 10 lakh
content: A Kerala consumer commission ordered Qatar Airways to pay ten lakh rupees compensation.  The airline denied boarding to two children, including an infant, despite issuing boarding passes.  This action was deemed a deficiency in service and unfair trade practice by the commission.  The family suffered significant mental agony and hardship due to the airline's arbitrary decision.  Qatar Airways must comply with the order within forty-five days.

title: KMRL urged to repair Kochi metro diversion roads, ease bottlenecks
content: People’s representatives cite potholes, damaged road shoulders and worsening congestion on key diversion routes; residents seek widening of Vennala High School Junction and removal of encroachments from footpaths; metro agency says it has sought ₹7.5 crore from the State government to repair diversion roads and streamline traffic

title: Farmers breach Baldota factory premises during protest in Koppal
content: A protest organised as part of the 46th Farmers’ Martyrs’ Day turned tense near the Baldota factory on the Koppal-Bengaluru National Highway on Tuesday after hundreds of farmers breached the factory premises by breaking sections of the compound wall and damaging the main gate

title: Congress surprises Govt, its own MPs with protest at PM's doorstep; thousands regroup at CJP's Jantar Mantar protest site | LIVE
content: Govt wasted our time, says Dipke on talks between Nadda, CJP team; Sonam Wangchuk shifted to Medanta Hospital

title: India-U.K. FTA creates predictability for businesses on both sides: City of London policy chief
content: The India-U.K. trade deal came into effect on July 15, 2026. In a written interview with The Hindu, Chris Hayward, Policy Chairman of the City of London Corporation, the U.K.’s financial and commercial hub, discusses the implications of the agreement, including what it means for manufacturing, infrastructure and financial services, as well as how investors can navigate changes in U.K. political leadership.

title: AAP accuses Congress of trying to weaken CJP-led protest
content: Former Delhi Chief Minister Atishi questioned how Mr. Gandhi was “allowed” to protest outside the PM’s residence while the CJP demonstration was being suppressed; the party accused the BJP and Congress of being ‘one unit’

title: ADGP M.R. Ajith Kumar submits explanation in YC workers assault case
content: The senior officer has reportedly denied all assessments made against him by the SIT that probed the incident and is handling the case

title: Idukki district administration plans to set up CSTP plant to prevent wastewater from flowing into Periyar lake
content: Without a proper treatment system, untreated wastewater from tourism establishments flows into a canal that feeds directly into the Periyar lake within the Mullaperiyar reservoir. Local body officials finds that several businesses were discharging wastewater directly into culverts connected to the canal

title: VIT partners with venture capital firms to invest in student startups
content: VIT signs MoU with Singapore-based Thinkuvate and Indore-based ValleyNXT, both institutional early stage venture capital firms, to support and fund student led startups

title: Hospitals must not function as an extension of law enforcement: Safdarjung Hospital resident doctors
content: Residents association seeks President’s intervention in Wangchuk case; defending patients’ rights, doctors say hospitals must not be seen as places of detention or used to facilitate non-medical objective

title: Atchannaidu urges greater central support for agriculture in Andhra Pradesh
content: In a series of meetings with Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan, and Union Ministers Shivraj Singh Chouhan and Srinivasa Varma, the Agriculture Minister explains the coalition government’s initiatives to strengthen the sector, improve farmers’ incomes and accelerate economic growth

title: Sensitivity of protest venue, stature of LoP led to quicker talks, but Rahul changed goalposts: Govt
content: Union Minister of State for the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) Jitendra Singh, the government’s interlocutor, met LoP Rahul Gandhi at the sit-in; Mr. Singh says Mr. Gandhi initially agreed to suspend the dharna but later also raised the demand of Dharmendra Pradhan’s ouster as Education Minister; to this, Mr. Singh said it did ‘behove an LoP to change stance’

title: Will keep reminding Centre of its promise on statehood: Omar
content: Underlining the importance of statehood, Omar Abdullah says it is only between a State and the Centre that “delineation and demarcation of powers could be discussed”

title: Avoid adding sugar or jaggery to Amrutham Nutrimix when cooking: WCD
content: Since Nutrimix already contains 20% sugar, avoid adding more sugar or jaggery when using Nutrimix to make ‘kozhukatta,’ ‘puttu,’ ‘ada,’ or other dishes in the interests of children’s health, it says

title: Local bodies identified as human-wildlife conflict zones in Kerala under risk assessment
content: A preliminary assessment that has mapped panchayats and municipalities in terms of risks associated with wildlife incursions and attacks has classified nine local bodies as very high conflict-prone areas, 21 as high and remaining 243 as medium conflict-prone zones

title: YSRCP petitions Election Commission over cross-State voter duplication
content: The YSRCP demands postponement of booth rationalisation in Andhra Pradesh until the publication of the final electoral roll

title: Martyrs' Day rally: Bury differences and unite against BJP, Mamata tells INDIA bloc parties
content: She extends olive branch to Opposition at Martyrs’ Day rally, but makes it clear that Trinamool rebels will never be taken back

title: Andhra Pradesh Tourism bags ‘Best Wedding Destination Emerging State’ award
content: Tourism Minister Kandula Durgesh receives the award at the International Wedding and Tourism Conclave in Jaipur; Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan says it reflects the tourism potential of the State and is a testament to the government’s vision towards promoting State as a premier tourism destination

title: Meditation offers stress relief, inner peace, says Swami Smaranananda
content: Delivered a lecture on meditation, the Yogoda Satsanga Society of India vice-president explains how scientifically designed exercises help recharge the body with life energy

title: Udayagiri wooden cutlery, Etikoppaka toys among 99 ODOP products from Andhra Pradesh, Union Minister tells Lok Sabha
content: Several steps have been taken to enhance the visibility of the products, Union Minister Jitin Prasada says in response to a question raised by Nellore MP Vemireddy Prabhakar Reddy

title: APSSDC launches campaign to boost participation in IndiaSkills Competition 2026-27
content: IndiaSkills Competition is India’s premier skills championship, designed to identify and nurture exceptional talent across a wide range of 63 vocational and technical trades

title: Anantapur set to become garbage-free with new waste processing plant
content: Inspecting the municipal dumping yard on Gooty road, SACL Chairman and Anantapur Urban MLA say Swachh Andhra Corporation is set to establish a plant capable of processing 170 metric tonnes of waste daily at a cost of ₹17.50 crore

title: 'Deeply insensitive': CJP removes spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya over burger video
content: “We strongly condemn the deeply insensitive actions of our spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya, whose videos surfaced while peaceful protestors and our team were facing brutal police violence,” the CJP statement said

title: Document writers protest against alleged privatisation of registration services
content: They sit in protest at the Sub Registrar’s office in Proddatur, announce that they will not extend their services until the end of the month; YSRCP leader expresses solidarity, flays State government for allegedly privatising every available service

title: Application window for Telangana govt’s affordable flats in Hyderabad now active; deadline is August 10
content: The housing application service was not active on MeeSeva WhatsApp number 8096958096 till July 21 evening at least

title: Six months of UDF rule in Kerala’s Pala municipality ends with ouster of chairperson Diya Binu
content: No-confidence motion, moved by LDF against Diya Binu, regarded as the youngest chairperson of any civic body in the country, passed with support of 17 councillors, including four Congress members and UDF vice-chairperson

title: GoM to examine phased shift of bulk cargo from Visakhapatnam Port to Mulapeta Port
content: Proposal aims to reduce pollution, ease congestion at Visakhapatnam Port and enable commercially viable operations at Mulapeta Port, says Minister

title: Shivamogga Deputy Commissioner holds meeting on forming Bharat Jodo Yuva Sanghas
content: Shivamogga Deputy Commissioner Prabhulinga Kavalikatti tells officers that the State government had planned to form 10,000 Bharat Jodo Yuvaka Sanghas in the State.

title: Video: Rahul, Priyanka Gandhi Detained From Protest Site, Taken Away In Bus
content: Congress workers, led by Rahul Gandhi, Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi, marched to PM Modi's residence to protest the paper leak issue on Tuesday.

title: Rahul Gandhi Went Back On His Word After His Demand Was Accepted: Minister
content: Singh said the Centre agreed to the Congress' demand for discussions in Parliament, but another clause was added by Gandhi.

title: Woman's Body Found Stuffed In Suitcase In Bengaluru Lake
content: According to preliminary information, local residents spotted the suitcase floating in the lake and alerted the police.

title: Rajasthan Man Kills Wife, Hides Body In Trunk After She Refuses To Give Money For Liquor
content: Station House Officer Ram Singh Yadav said the accused, Krishna Kumar Jat (40), had a dispute with his wife Sulochana (34) on Saturday over money for alcohol.

title: Karnataka Cabinet Expansion Tomorrow: New Faces, Women Likely To Get Berths
content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar and Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) President BK Hariprasad reached the national capital on Tuesday for consultations with the party's central leadership.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Shifted From Safdarjung Hospital To Medanta After Court Order
content: The court asked the director of Medanta Hospital to form a team of doctors with requisite expertise to constantly monitor Wangchuk.

title: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: Congress Leaders Removed From Protest Site Outside PM's House
content: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi protested outside PM Modi's house. He and other leaders of his party were later detained.

title: AAP's Swipe As Rahul Gandhi Stages Sit-In Protest Outside PM Modi's Home
content: Rahul Gandhi's protest outside the PM's house is an attempt to undermine the protests by the Cockroach Janta Party, the AAP claimed.

title: Shipowner Offers Sailors 6-Fold Pay To Cross Strait Of Hormuz: Report
content: According to a document seen by Bloomberg, a company offered seafarers an additional six months' salary if they completed a round trip through the strait to collect crude oil from Saudi Arabia or Iraq and unload it in the Gulf of Oman.

title: Adani Total Gas Q1 Revenue Rises 27% To Rs 1,910 Crore
content: The company said its operational performance remained strong during the quarter, with combined standalone CNG and PNG sales volumes rising 13 per cent year-on-year to 303 million metric standard cubic metres (MMSCM).

title: Man Accused Of Killing 6 Child Welfare Workers In Germany Found Dead In Jail
content: The suspect, who has been partially identified in German media as 45-year-old Fatih Khan G., was found dead by jail staff in his single cell early Tuesday.

title: 10 Arrested for Attacking Civic Officials During Bengaluru Eviction Drive
content: Violence erupted during an anti-footpath encroachment drive in Bengaluru's Shivajinagar after street vendors allegedly attacked Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) officials who had arrived to clear encroachments from public footpaths.

title: Soy Milk, Pasta And A Prison Plea: US Mercenary Wins Food Relief In Tihar Jail
content: Counsel for Matthew VanDyke argued at length before the court that his client had been unable to eat the prison diet because it was "spicy, oily, deep-fried and greasy", and that his medical condition required a different diet.

title: Army Rescues 571 People From Flood-Hit Areas In Assam
content: Dedicated Flood Relief Columns from the Spear Corps have been carrying out day-and-night rescue operation since on Jul 19, officials said.

title: Kuwait Says Iran Hit Several Power, Water Desalination Plants
content: Kuwait's ministry of electricity confirmed fires at several facilities due to repeated attacks by Iran over four days.

title: 20-Year-Old Woman In ICU After Being Injured At CJP Protests In Delhi
content: The woman, who remains on ventilator support, collided with a barricade during police crackdown on protesters at Jantar Mantar.

title: CJP Sacks Spokesperson Over Burger Video. "I'm Not Accountable," He Says
content: The CJP said in a statement that it "strongly condemns the deeply insensitive actions" of Vijeta Dahiya, whose videos surfaced on social media during Monday's march.

title: Ex-Cop, His Family Beat Farmer To Death Over Land Dispute In Rajasthan
content: The accused, a neighbouring retired ASI, wanted to possess the land and was pressuring the victim, Sushil to transfer it in his name.

title: 70 km To 500 Metres: Two New Ladakh Bridges To Boost Border Tourism
content: A new motorable bridge in Ladakh's Nubra Valley is expected to reduce a nearly 70-km journey to a crossing of about 500 metres, making remote border villages easier to reach for residents and tourists.

title: US education groups slam DHS rule ending 'duration of status' for int'l students
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. content: Russia USA USA Russia France India N. title: US student visa rules are changing on September 15: Who could be affected and how
content: the changes are aimed at curbing visa abuse, strengthening security checks and bringing greater federal oversight to international students . the new rules introduce a maximum four-year stay for most students, shorten post-study grace period and require government approval for extensions beyond the authorised stay . more than 3.6 lakh Indians were enrolled in American universities during the 2024-25 academic year .

title: New US visa rule gives OPT students temporary extension filing exemption
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More
